name: Magaña, Castilfrío
name_original: Magaña, Castilfrío de la Sierra y Narros — circular desde Garray
description: This difficult 52.5-mile road cycling route from Garray explores the scenic Soria province, passing through Narros, Magaña, and Castilfrío d
region_intro: Road cycling around Garray, a municipality in Soria, Spain, features diverse landscapes suitable for various fitness levels. The region is characterized by significant natural features such as the Sierra Cebollera Natural Park and the Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo reservoir. Road cycling routes often include notable elevation gains and pass through areas of natural beauty and historical interest. The terrain offers a mix of well-paved surfaces and some unpaved segments, providing varied cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Garray The most popular road cycling route is Piqueras and Santa Inés Pass Loop, a 70.8 miles (113.9 km) trail that takes 5 hours 42 minutes to complete. This difficult route traverses the Sierra Cebollera Natural Park, featuring substantial elevation gains. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Yacimiento Arqueológico de Numancia y Ribera del Duro — circular desde Sória, a moderate 32.9 miles (53.0 km) path. This route passes the Archaeological Site of Numancia and follows the Ribera del Duero. Local road cyclists also love the Garray and Embalse de la cuerda del Pozo — circular por la Sierra de Carcaña, a 52.3 miles (84.1 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Carcaña and past the Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo, often completed in about 3 hours 41 minutes. Road cycling around Garray is defined by diverse landscapes, significant elevation changes, and natural features like the Sierra Cebollera Natural Park and the Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Garray are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from 15 reviews. More than 800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Garray's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of road cycling routes are available around Garray?
A: Garray offers a diverse range of road cycling routes, catering to various fitness levels. You'll find everything from easier rides to challenging routes with significant elevation gains. The terrain often features a mix of well-paved surfaces, though some segments may be unpaved, adding to the varied experience.

Q: How many road cycling routes are there in the Garray area?
A: There are over 50 road cycling routes available around Garray, with options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. This includes 3 easy routes, 36 moderate routes, and 12 difficult routes, ensuring a suitable ride for almost every cyclist.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, experienced cyclists looking for a challenge will find several difficult routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1071173668/piqueras-and-santa-ines-pass-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piqueras and Santa Inés Pass Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 70.8-mile circular route through the Sierra Cebollera Natural Park, featuring substantial elevation gains. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079299121/magana-castilfrio" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magaña, Castilfrío&lt;/a&gt; route, which covers over 52 miles with significant climbing.

Q: Can I find easier road cycling routes suitable for beginners or a more relaxed ride?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a less strenuous ride, there are easier options available. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1078460346/from-soria-to-garray-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Sória to Garray — circular&lt;/a&gt; route, which is a shorter, more manageable ride, perfect for beginners or a leisurely outing.

Q: What natural attractions can I expect to see along the road cycling routes?
A: The region around Garray is rich in natural beauty. Many routes pass through or offer views of the stunning Sierra Cebollera Natural Park and the scenic Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo (reservoir). You might also encounter features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2999663" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de la Toba&lt;/a&gt; waterfall or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4077489" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Golmayo River&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks to explore while cycling near Garray?
A: Yes, the area is steeped in history. A notable landmark is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5419216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Numantia Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt;, famous for its role in the Celtiberian Wars, which some routes pass by. Other historical points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3402819" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Saturio&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5258521" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of San Juan de Duero&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available around Garray?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Garray are circular, allowing you to start and end your ride in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1071173668/piqueras-and-santa-ines-pass-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piqueras and Santa Inés Pass Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1256274373/garray-and-embalse-de-la-cuerda-del-pozo-circular-por-la-sierra-de-carcana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garray and Embalse de la cuerda del Pozo — circular por la Sierra de Carcaña&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Garray?
A: The road cycling routes in Garray are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.8 out of 5 stars from 15 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, significant elevation changes, and the natural beauty of areas like the Sierra Cebollera Natural Park and the Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by the Ribera del Duero?
A: Yes, some routes offer the opportunity to cycle along the scenic Ribera del Duero. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1070344737/yacimiento-arqueologico-de-numancia-y-ribera-del-duro-circular-desde-soria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yacimiento Arqueológico de Numancia y Ribera del Duro — circular desde Sória&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that takes you past the Archaeological Site of Numancia and follows the Ribera del Duero.

Q: Are there any routes that go through the Sierra de Carcaña?
A: Indeed. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1256274373/garray-and-embalse-de-la-cuerda-del-pozo-circular-por-la-sierra-de-carcana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garray and Embalse de la cuerda del Pozo — circular por la Sierra de Carcaña&lt;/a&gt; is a popular route that leads through the picturesque Sierra de Carcaña, offering beautiful views and a rewarding cycling experience.
tips: Population located north of the Sierra del Almuerzo and close to the entrance of the Iberian System on the Sória side.
Before the entrance to the town of Magaña there is an old bridge that crosses the Río Alhama and from where you can see the whole of the Castle that is at the top of the town.
This small town in the Almarza region is located in the north of the province, bathed by the Chico river, a tributary of the Merdancho in the Duero basin, south of the Rodader mountain range

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Garray, making it a convenient base. While specific designated cyclist parking isn't detailed, Garray is a small town, and you should be able to find suitable parking spots within the village, especially near the starting point of the circular route.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as difficult. With a distance of over 84 km and an elevation gain of approximately 1220 meters, it is best suited for experienced road cyclists looking for a challenging ride through varied terrain in the Soria province.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Soria province generally offers good cycling conditions during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is particularly vibrant. Summer can be hot, and winter may bring colder temperatures and potential snow in higher elevations.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. The roads are public, and the area is part of the Tierras Altas de Soria, which does not have general entry fees for outdoor activities like cycling.

faqs.question: What points of interest will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: This route takes you through several charming locations. You'll pass through the settlement of Narros, the historic Magaña Bridge, and the picturesque village of Castilfrío de la Sierra. The route offers a blend of natural beauty and cultural sights typical of the Soria region.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, characteristic of the Soria province. You can expect rolling hills, climbs, and descents as you navigate through different landscapes. The roads are generally quiet with low traffic density, offering a tranquil cycling experience. The route covers a mix of riverbanks and more elevated areas, providing diverse challenges and scenic views.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally welcome in outdoor areas, this is a long and challenging road cycling route. It's not typically suitable for dogs to accompany cyclists for the entire distance. If you plan to bring a dog, consider shorter, off-road sections or ensure your dog can safely travel in a trailer designed for cycling.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this road cycling route briefly overlap with the Camino Natural del Agua Soriano. While the primary focus is road cycling, you'll experience small parts of this official trail along the way.

faqs.question: Are there places to find food or accommodation along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through several villages like Magaña, Castilfrío de la Sierra, and Narros, as well as starting in Garray. These settlements may offer limited options for cafes, restaurants, or small accommodations. It's advisable to check availability in advance, especially outside of peak season, and carry sufficient water and snacks for the duration of your ride.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while cycling?
faqs.answer: You can expect diverse and beautiful scenery. The Soria province is known for its natural beauty, including picturesque views of natural rock formations, charming villages, and verdant landscapes. The route may also offer glimpses of the Duero River and historical sites, providing a rich visual experience.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this difficult road cycling route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, you should bring appropriate gear. This includes a well-maintained road bike, helmet, cycling apparel, sufficient water (at least 2-3 liters), energy snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), navigation device, and sun protection. Layers of clothing are recommended due to potential temperature changes.
